assertions.text: Response subtracts the origin offset (400, 300) from the pixel coordinate before converting
Response uses the flat-top pixel-to-hex formula: q = (2/3 * x) / size, r = (-1/3 * x + sqrt(3)/3 * y) / size
assertions.evidence: Step 2 shows exact formula with labels, then substitutes values correctly

assertions.text: Response uses cube rounding (resets the component with the largest rounding error) rather than rounding each component independently
assertions.evidence: Step 3 explicitly computes s_diff=0.4040 as largest, resets s=-q-r, verifies invariant holds

assertions.text: Response arrives at the correct hex: axial q=1, r=-2 (equivalently cube q=1, r=-2, s=1)
assertions.evidence: Final answer: 'The player clicked hex (q = 1, r = -2)' with cube q=1, r=-2, s=1 stated
